description: This figure presents a temperature-time profile schematic illustrating the isothermal constant strain rate compression experimental procedure for FeeMneAleC (Fe-Mn-Al-C) steel conducted on a Gleeble-3500 thermo-mechanical simulator. The thermal cycle comprises heating to 1200°C at 10°C/s, homogenization for 120 s, cooling at 5°C/s to the target deformation temperature (ranging from 900°C to 1150°C per the experimental matrix), a 15 s holding period to ensure thermal equilibrium, followed by compression at constant strain rates (0.01–10 s⁻¹) with 60% reduction (true strain 0.92), and final water quenching. The diagram explicitly labels the heating rate, homogenization conditions, cooling rate, holding time, compression stage, and quenching step, providing a complete thermal processing route for investigating the dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of the low-density high-strength steel under hot deformation conditions.
